introduction: A disorder characterized by episodic attacks of extreme fear, mostly unrelated to specific objects or situations. Associated with multiple somatic and cognitive symptoms. Each attack lasts for about 5 - 30 minutes. Often begins abruptly. Affects about 0.5 - 1.0% of the population

features: tingling or numbness in the hands or feet

investigations: As indicated to exclude medical aetiologies

goals: Reduce intensity and frequency of attacks
drug: Fluoxetine: Adult: initially 20 mg orally once daily, increased after two weeks (if necessary) to 20 - 60 mg once daily (maximum 80 mg); Elderly: 20 - 40 mg (maximum 60 mg for elderly) once daily; Discontinue in both adults and elderly if no improvement within 10 weeks; Child and adolescent under 18 years: not recommended
adverse_reactions_and_cautions: Tricyclic antidepressants are cardio-toxic in overdose
Increased risk of suicidal attempts by patients with panic disorder

differential_diagnoses: Other causes of intense fear (phobias, obsessive-compulsive disorders, etc.)
Medical causes (e.g. hyperthyroid states, episodic hypoglycaemia, etc.)
